content:
Hi,

As a learning experience, I am looking into converting some self contained code within an existing driver to Rust. For this, I want to implement a non static function in Rust. Is this possible currently?

One approach is being able to call a rust function from a C (wrapper from C for calling the rust implementation). I tried this, with #[no_mangle] pub extern "C" on the rust implementation, but the build system isnt able to detect it. Are there any solutions ?

Generally, we avoid "function-by-function" conversions like that and mixing C and Rust in modules etc.

If you really want to do it, for a module you will probably need to create a "mixed C and Rust" one, see samples/rust/rust_print* as an example.

thanks for the response! I'm doing this in amdgpu driver, and thats too big to be converted to rust fully, so as a learning exercise, its more feasible for me to try to convert isolated parts of the driver to Rust. The rust_print example seems to be calling the C fn rust_sample_loaded from Rust. I'm looking for the reverse - call Rust fn from C code.
I meant it as an example on how to build two object files (one in C, one in Rust) into a single module, since the problem you had seemed to be that you weren't able to link.
i.e. are you getting "undefined reference" or similar, or is it something else?
Doesnt seem to be a link error - its undeclared function so compile error
In C? Are you providing the declaration?
Oh maybe I just need the forward declaration. Let me try that
Hmm that seems to have worked. Silly me. Thank you nevertheless

At some point we will likely have better support for that case, but for the moment you need to manually provide it.

You're welcome!
Jayaura has marked this topic as resolved.
